name: Pastel Tres Leches Aldaco
recipeIngredient: 1 can whole milk, (use empty condensed milk can)
Chopped pecans -- or cinnamon to sprinkle on top
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly grease a 9-inch round cake pan.
Mix together flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt in a medium bowl.
In another bowl, stir together the eggs, milk, and vanilla lightly.
Mix with the dry ingredients and stir well until no lumps remain.
Scrape batter into the cake pan and bake for about 20 minutes or until
the top is light golden and springs back when touched in the center.
Remove from oven and let cool on a rack for 15 minutes. Then remove from
pan so it doesn't stick; allow to cool completely.
When cooled, cut horizontally with a long-bladed knife into two equal
pieces.
To make the milk mixture: combine evaporated milk, sweetened condensed
milk, whole milk (from an empty can), vanilla, and rum (optional) in a
large bowl or blender. Blend until smooth.
Lift one piece of cake aside; place the other piece on a larger serving
dish with a rim to catch excess liquid.
Slowly pour enough of the milk mixture over the bottom half to fully
soak it. Replace the top piece and continue pouring more mixture until
the entire cake is soaked, adding more as needed for absorption.
Allow to soak in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours (overnight is best).
Cut into pie-shaped wedges before serving. Garnish each wedge with
chopped pecans or a sprinkle of cinnamon if desired.
Any leftover milk can be poured into a pitcher and served alongside the
dessert.
